meaning: n.pl.未开垦地,(偏僻的森林地带)
examples: I'm thinking about accepting the offer from Backwoods Hospital.
The backwoods folks and the swamp dwellers owned neither horses nor mules.
Let me tell you about a little girl who was born into a very poor family in a shack in the Backwoods of Tennessee.
Over the backwoods and fields which, given the time of year, were typically covered in snow.
And the gamble paid off: Britons of the time thirsted for images of backwoods America.
